records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.328 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"Whoever grows their hair and does not part it, Allah (swt) will part it with a saw of fire."
records.arabicText: 328 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «مَنِ اِتَّخَذَ شَعْراً فَلَمْ يَفْرُقْهُ فَرَقَهُ اَللَّهُ بِمِنْشَارٍ مِنْ نَارٍ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.328 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever grows their hair and does not part it, Allah (swt) will part it with a saw of fire."

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.329 - The Messenger of Allah (sw) said: "Trim your mustaches and let your beards grow, and do not resemble the Jews."
records.arabicText: 329 - وَقَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ: «حُفُّوا اَلشَّوَارِبَ وَ أَعْفُوا اَللِّحَى وَ لاَ تَشَبَّهُوا بِالْيَهُودِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.329 - The Messenger of Allah (sw) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Trim your mustaches and let your beards grow, and do not resemble the Jews."

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.330 - "The Messenger of Allah (sw) once saw (looked) a man with a long beard, and said: "It would have been better if he had trimmed his beard.
When the man heard this, he trimmed his beard to a moderate length. Then entered to see the Prophet. When the Prophet saw him, He (sw) said: "This is how you should do it. "
records.arabicText: 330 - وَ نَظَرَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ إِلَى رَجُلٍ طَوِيلِ اَللِّحْيَةِ فَقَالَ «مَا كَانَ هَذَا لَوْ هَيَّأَ مِنْ لِحْيَتِهِ » فَبَلَغَ اَلرَّجُلَ ذَلِكَ فَهَيَّأَ مِنْ لِحْيَتِهِ بَيْنَ اَللِّحْيَتَيْنِ ثُمَّ دَخَلَ عَلَى اَلنَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ فَلَمَّا رَآهُ قَالَ «هَكَذَا فَافْعَلُوا.

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.331 - The Messenger of Allah (sw) said: "The Magians (Zoroastrians) trim their beards and let their mustaches grow long, but we trim the mustaches and let the beards grow, and this is the natural disposition (fitrah)."
records.arabicText: 331 - وَقَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ: «إِنَّ اَلْمَجُوسَ جَزُّوا لِحَاهُمْ وَ وَفَّرُوا شَوَارِبَهُمْ وَ إِنَّا نَجُزُّ اَلشَّوَارِبَ وَ نُعْفِي اَللِّحَى وَ هِيَ اَلْفِطْرَةُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.331 - The Messenger of Allah (sw) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The Magians (Zoroastrians) trim their beards and let their mustaches grow long, but we trim the mustaches and let the beards grow, and this is the natural disposition (fitrah)."

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.332 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said: "Whatever extends beyond a fist-length of the beard is in the Fire."
records.arabicText: 332 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «مَا زَادَ مِنَ اَللِّحْيَةِ عَنْ قَبْضَةٍ فَهُوَ فِي اَلنَّارِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.332 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whatever extends beyond a fist-length of the beard is in the Fire."

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.333 - Muhammad ibn Muslim said: "I saw Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) while the barber was trimming his beard, and Imam (as) said: 'Shape it evenly.'
records.arabicText: 333 - وَقَالَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ: رَأَيْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ اَلْبَاقِرَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ وَ اَلْحَجَّامُ يَأْخُذُ مِنْ لِحْيَتِهِ فَقَالَ «دَوِّرْهَا.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.333 - Muhammad ibn Muslim said: "I saw Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) while the barber was trimming his beard, and Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: 'Shape it evenly.'

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.334 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said: "Hold your beard with your hand (in a fist) and trim what is excess."
records.arabicText: 334 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «تَقْبِضُ بِيَدِكَ عَلَى لِحْيَتِكَ وَ تَجُزُّ مَا فَضَلَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.334 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Hold your beard with your hand (in a fist) and trim what is excess."

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.335 - The Messenger of Allah (sw) said:
"Gray hair at the front of the head is a sign of blessing, at the temples is a sign of generosity, at the hair ends is a sign of courage, and at the nape of the neck is a sign of ill-omen."
records.arabicText: 335 - وَقَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ: «اَلشَّيْبُ فِي مُقَدَّمِ اَلرَّأْسِ يُمْنٌ وَ فِي اَلْعَارِضَيْنِ سَخَاءٌ وَ فِي اَلذَّوَائِبِ شَجَاعَةٌ وَ فِي اَلْقَفَا شُؤْمٌ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.335 - The Messenger of Allah (sw) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Gray hair at the front of the head is a sign of blessing, at the temples is a sign of generosity, at the hair ends is a sign of courage, and at the nape of the neck is a sign of ill-omen."

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.336 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said: "The first person to have gray hair was Ibrahim (as), the Friend (Khaleel) of Allah (swt). When he saw a white strand in his beard, He (as) said:
'O’ Archangel Jibril (as), what is this?'
Archangel Jibril (as) said: "'This is dignity (Waqar).'
So Ibrahim (as) replied: 'O’ Allah (swt), increase me in dignity.'
records.arabicText: 336 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «أَوَّلُ مَنْ شَابَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ اَلْخَلِيلُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ وَ إِنَّهُ ثَنَى لِحْيَتَهُ فَرَأَى طَاقَةً بَيْضَاءَ فَقَالَ يَا جَبْرَئِيلُ مَا هَذَا فَقَالَ هَذَا وَقَارٌ فَقَالَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ اَللَّهُمَّ زِدْنِي وَقَاراً.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.336 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"The first person to have gray hair was Ibrahim (as), the Friend (Khaleel) of Allah (swt). When he saw a white strand in his beard, He (as) said:
'O’ Archangel Jibril (as), what is this?'
Archangel Jibril (as) said: "'This is dignity (Waqar).'
So Ibrahim (as) replied: 'O’ Allah (swt), increase me in dignity.'

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.337 - Imam (as) said: "Whoever grows a gray hair in Islam, it will be a light for them on the Day of Judgment."
records.arabicText: 337 - وَقَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «مَنْ شَابَ شَيْبَةً فِي اَلْإِسْلاَمِ كَانَتْ لَهُ نُوراً يَوْمَ اَلْقِيَامَةِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.337 - Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever grows a gray hair in Islam, it will be a light for them on the Day of Judgment."

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.338 - The Messenger of Allah (sw) said: "Gray hair is light, so do not pluck it."
records.arabicText: 338 - وَقَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ: «اَلشَّيْبُ نُورٌ فَلاَ تَنْتِفُوهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.338 - The Messenger of Allah (sw) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Gray hair is light, so do not pluck it."

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.339 - "It is reported that Imam Ali (as) saw no harm in cutting gray hair but disliked plucking it."
records.arabicText: 339 - «وَ كَانَ عَلِيٌّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ لاَ يَرَى بِجَزِّ اَلشَّيْبِ بَأْساً وَ يَكْرَهُ نَتْفَهُ.

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.340 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) says: "There is no harm in cutting gray hair or plucking it, but cutting it is more beloved to me than plucking it."
records.arabicText:
340 - اَلصَّادِقَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يَقُولُ: «لاَ بَأْسَ بِجَزِّ اَلشَّمَطِ وَ نَتْفِهِ وَ جَزُّهُ أَحَبُّ إِلَيَّ مِنْ نَتْفِهِ.
فأخبارهم عليهم السلام لا تختلف في حالة واحدة لان مخرجها من عند الله تعالى ذكره، وإنما تختلف بحسب اختلاف الأحوال.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.340 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) says:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no harm in cutting gray hair or plucking it, but cutting it is more beloved to me than plucking it."

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.341 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"There are four traits of the Prophets: using fragrance, grooming with a razor, shaving the body with Nuura (depilatory paste), and frequenting the mosque."
records.arabicText: 341 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «أَرْبَعٌ مِنْ أَخْلاَقِ اَلْأَنْبِيَاءِ عَلَيْهِمُ اَلسَّلاَمُ اَلتَّطَيُّبُ وَ اَلتَّنْظِيفُ بِالْمُوسَى وَ حَلْقُ اَلْجَسَدِ بِالنُّورَةِ وَ كَثْرَةُ اَلطَّرُوقَةِ.»
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.341 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There are four traits of the Prophets: using fragrance, grooming with a razor, shaving the body with Nuura (depilatory paste), and frequenting the mosque."

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.342 - Imam (as) said: "Trim your nails on Tuesday, bathe on Wednesday, fulfil your need for cupping (hijama) on Thursday, and use your best fragrance on Jumu’ah."
records.arabicText: 342 - وَقَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «قَلِّمُوا أَظْفَارَكُمْ يَوْمَ اَلثَّلاَثَاءِ وَ اِسْتَحِمُّوا يَوْمَ اَلْأَرْبِعَاءِ ' وَ أَصِيبُوا مِنَ اَلْحِجَامَةِ حَاجَتَكُمْ يَوْمَ اَلْخَمِيسِ وَ تَطَيَّبُوا بِأَطْيَبِ طِيبِكُمْ يَوْمَ اَلْجُمُعَةِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.342 - Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Trim your nails on Tuesday, bathe on Wednesday, fulfil your need for cupping (hijama) on Thursday, and use your best fragrance on Jumu’ah."

records.chapter: Washing of the Deceased
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.396 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"Do not leave your deceased alone, for Shaytan may disturb them within their body."
records.arabicText: 396 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «لاَ تَدَعَنَّ مَيِّتَكَ وَحْدَهُ فَإِنَّ اَلشَّيْطَانَ يَعْبَثُ بِهِ فِي جَوْفِهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.396 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Do not leave your deceased alone, for Shaytan may disturb them within their body."

records.chapter: Washing of the Deceased
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.343 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said: "The Messenger of Allah (sw) entered upon a man from the Banu Hashim who was in the throes of death and said to him: 'Say :
La ilaha illa Allah al-Haleemu al-Kareem, la ilaha illa Allah al-‘Aliyyu al-‘Azheem. Subhana Allah Rabbi as-samawati as-sab‘ wa Rabbi al-ardhina as-sab‘ wa ma feehinna wa ma baynahunna wa ma tahtahunna wa Rabbi al-‘Arsh al-‘Azheem. Wa salamun ‘ala al-mursaleen, wal-hamdu lillahi Rabb al ‘alameen
'There is no Allah (swt) but Allah (swt), the Forbearing, the Generous; there is no God but Allah (swt), the Most High, the Most Great; Glory be to Allah (swt), the Lord (azj) of the seven heavens and the Lord (azj) of the seven earths and what is in them and what is between them and what is beneath them, and the Lord (azj) of the Mighty Throne. Peace be upon the messengers, and all praise is due to Allah (swt), the Lord (azj) of all the worlds.'
The man said thusly, and the Messenger of Allah (sw) said: 'Praise be to Allah (swt) who has saved him from the Fire.'".
records.arabicText: 343 - قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «إِنَّ رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ دَخَلَ عَلَى رَجُلٍ مِنْ بَنِي هَاشِمٍ وَ هُوَ فِي اَلنَّزْعِ فَقَالَ لَهُ «قُلْ: لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اَللَّهُ اَلْحَلِيمُ اَلْكَرِيمُ لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اَللَّهُ اَلْعَلِيُّ اَلْعَظِيمُ سُبْحَانَ اَللَّهِ رَبِّ اَلسَّمَاوَاتِ اَلسَّبْعِ وَ رَبِّ اَلْأَرَضِينَ اَلسَّبْعِ وَ مَا فِيهِنَّ وَ مَا بَيْنَهُنَّ وَ مَا تَحْتَهُنَّ وَ رَبِّ اَلْعَرْشِ اَلْعَظِيمِ «وَ سَلاٰمٌ عَلَى اَلْمُرْسَلِينَ `وَ اَلْحَمْدُ لِلّٰهِ رَبِّ اَلْعٰالَمِينَ» » فَقَالَهَا فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ « اَلْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ اَلَّذِي أَنْقَذَهُ مِنَ اَلنَّارِ » »
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.343 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"The Messenger of Allah (sw) entered upon a man from the Banu Hashim who was in the throes of death and said to him: 'Say :
La ilaha illa Allah al-Haleemu al-Kareem, la ilaha illa Allah al-‘Aliyyu al-‘Azheem. Subhana Allah Rabbi as-samawati as-sab‘ wa Rabbi al-ardhina as-sab‘ wa ma feehinna wa ma baynahunna wa ma tahtahunna wa Rabbi al-‘Arsh al-‘Azheem. Wa salamun ‘ala al-mursaleen, wal-hamdu lillahi Rabb al ‘alameen
'There is no Allah (swt) but Allah (swt), the Forbearing, the Generous; there is no God but Allah (swt), the Most High, the Most Great; Glory be to Allah (swt), the Lord (azj) of the seven heavens and the Lord (azj) of the seven earths and what is in them and what is between them and what is beneath them, and the Lord (azj) of the Mighty Throne. Peace be upon the messengers, and all praise is due to Allah (swt), the Lord (azj) of all the worlds.'
The man said thusly, and the Messenger of Allah (sw) said: 'Praise be to Allah (swt) who has saved him from the Fire.'".

records.chapter: Washing of the Deceased
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.344 - Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said: "You instruct your deceased to say 'There is no god but Allah (swt)' at the time of death, while we instruct our deceased to say 'Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah (sw).'".
records.arabicText: 344 - وَقَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «إِنَّكُمْ تُلَقِّنُونَ مَوْتَاكُمْ: لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اَللَّهُ عِنْدَ اَلْمَوْتِ وَ نَحْنُ نُلَقِّنُ مَوْتَانَا مُحَمَّدٌ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ.

records.chapter: Washing of the Deceased
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.345 - The Messenger of Allah (sw) said: "Instruct your dying to say 'There is no god but Allah (swt),' for whoever's last words are 'There is no god but Allah (swt)' will enter Paradise."
records.arabicText: 345 - وَقَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ: «لَقِّنُوا مَوْتَاكُمْ لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اَللَّهُ فَإِنَّ مَنْ كَانَ آخِرُ كَلاَمِهِ لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اَللَّهُ دَخَلَ اَلْجَنَّةَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.345 - The Messenger of Allah (sw) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Instruct your dying to say 'There is no god but Allah (swt),' for whoever's last words are 'There is no god but Allah (swt)' will enter Paradise."

records.chapter: Washing of the Deceased
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.346 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said: "The believer is most rational (aware) at the time of their death."
records.arabicText: 346 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «أَعْقَلُ مَا يَكُونُ اَلْمُؤْمِنُ عِنْدَ مَوْتِهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.346 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The believer is most rational (aware) at the time of their death."

records.chapter: Washing of the Deceased
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.347 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said: "During the time of the Messenger of Allah (sw), a man from the people of Medina was unable to speak due to throes of death. The Messenger of Allah (sw) entered upon him and said:
'Say: There is no god but Allah (swt).' But the man was unable to say it.
The Messenger of Allah (sw) repeated it to him, but he still could not say it.
At the head of this man was a woman, and He asked her: 'Is this man’s mother present?'"
She said: 'Yes, O’ Messenger of Allah (sw), I am his mother.'
He asked her: 'Are you pleased with him or displeased?'
She said: 'I am displeased with him.'
The Messenger of Allah (sw) said to her: "I would like that you become pleased with him,"
She said: 'I am pleased with him for your sake, O’ Messenger of Allah (sw).'
He (sw) then said to him: "Say: There is no god but Allah (swt).'"
(the man) replied: "There is no god but Allah (swt)."
Then He (sw) said: 'Say: O’ One who accepts the small and forgives the great, accept from me the small and forgive the great, for You are the Most Forgiving, the Most Merciful.'
(the man) said it, and then He asked him, 'What do you see?'
(the man) replied: 'I see two dark figures approaching me.'
He (sw) said: 'Repeat the supplication.'
So he repeated it, and He (sw) said: 'What do you see now?'
(the man) replied: 'They have moved away from me, and two white figures have come in, and the dark figures have left. I no longer see them, and the white figures are now drawing close to me to take my soul.' The man then passed away thereafter.
records.arabicText: 347 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «اُعْتُقِلَ لِسَانُ رَجُلٍ مِنْ أَهْلِ اَلْمَدِينَةِ عَلَى عَهْدِ رَسُولِ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ فِي مَرَضِهِ اَلَّذِي مَاتَ فِيهِ فَدَخَلَ عَلَيْهِ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ فَقَالَ لَهُ «قُلْ لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اَللَّهُ » فَلَمْ يَقْدِرْ عَلَيْهِ فَأَعَادَ عَلَيْهِ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ فَلَمْ يَقْدِرْ عَلَيْهِ وَ عِنْدَ رَأْسِ اَلرَّجُلِ اِمْرَأَةٌ فَقَالَ لَهَا «هَلْ لِهَذَا اَلرَّجُلِ أُمٌّ» فَقَالَتْ نَعَمْ يَا رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ أَنَا أُمُّهُ فَقَالَ لَهَا «أَ فَرَاضِيَةٌ أَنْتِ عَنْهُ أَمْ لاَ» فَقَالَتْ لاَ بَلْ سَاخِطَةٌ فَقَالَ لَهَا رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ «فَإِنِّي أُحِبُّ أَنْ تَرْضَيْ عَنْهُ» فَقَالَتْ قَدْ رَضِيتُ عَنْهُ لِرِضَاكَ يَا رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ فَقَالَ لَهُ «قُلْ لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اَللَّهُ » فَقَالَ لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اَللَّهُ فَقَالَ «قُلْ يَا مَنْ يَقْبَلُ اَلْيَسِيرَ وَ يَعْفُو عَنِ اَلْكَثِيرِ اِقْبَلْ مِنِّي اَلْيَسِيرَ وَ اُعْفُ عَنِّي اَلْكَثِيرَ إِنَّكَ أَنْتَ اَلْعَفُوُّ اَلْغَفُورُ » فَقَالَهَا فَقَالَ لَهُ «مَا ذَا تَرَى» فَقَالَ أَرَى أَسْوَدَيْنِ قَدْ دَخَلاَ عَلَيَّ قَالَ «أَعِدْهَا» فَأَعَادَهَا فَقَالَ «مَا ذَا تَرَى» فَقَالَ قَدْ تَبَاعَدَا عَنِّي وَ دَخَلَ أَبْيَضَانِ وَ خَرَجَ اَلْأَسْوَدَانِ فَمَا أَرَاهُمَا وَ دَنَا اَلْأَبْيَضَانِ مِنِّي اَلْآنَ يَأْخُذَانِ بِنَفْسِي فَمَاتَ مِنْ سَاعَتِهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.347 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"During the time of the Messenger of Allah (sw), a man from the people of Medina was unable to speak due to throes of death. The Messenger of Allah (sw) entered upon him and said:
'Say: There is no god but Allah (swt).' But the man was unable to say it.
The Messenger of Allah (sw) repeated it to him, but he still could not say it.
At the head of this man was a woman, and He asked her: 'Is this man’s mother present?'"
She said: 'Yes, O’ Messenger of Allah (sw), I am his mother.'
He asked her: 'Are you pleased with him or displeased?'
She said: 'I am displeased with him.'
The Messenger of Allah (sw) said to her: "I would like that you become pleased with him,"
She said: 'I am pleased with him for your sake, O’ Messenger of Allah (sw).'
He (sw) then said to him: "Say: There is no god but Allah (swt).'"
(the man) replied: "There is no god but Allah (swt)."
Then He (sw) said: 'Say: O’ One who accepts the small and forgives the great, accept from me the small and forgive the great, for You are the Most Forgiving, the Most Merciful.'
(the man) said it, and then He asked him, 'What do you see?'
(the man) replied: 'I see two dark figures approaching me.'
He (sw) said: 'Repeat the supplication.'
So he repeated it, and He (sw) said: 'What do you see now?'
(the man) replied: 'They have moved away from me, and two white figures have come in, and the dark figures have left. I no longer see them, and the white figures are now drawing close to me to take my soul.' The man then passed away thereafter.

records.chapter: Washing of the Deceased
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.348 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) was asked about the positioning of the deceased, and Imam (as) said: "Direct the soles of their feet toward the Qibla”.
records.arabicText: 348 - وَ سُئِلَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ تَوْجِيهِ اَلْمَيِّتِ فَقَالَ «اِسْتَقْبِلْ بِبَاطِنِ قَدَمَيْهِ اَلْقِبْلَةَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.348 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) was asked about the positioning of the deceased, and Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Direct the soles of their feet toward the Qibla”.

records.chapter: Washing of the Deceased
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.349 - The Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said: "The Messenger of Allah (sw) entered upon a man from the descendants of Abdul Muttalib, while he was in the throes of death and was facing away from the Qibla.
The Prophet said: 'Direct him toward the Qibla, for when you do so, the angels will approach him, and Allah (swt), the Mighty and Glorious, will turn toward him with His face (attention).'
And he remained in that state until he passed away.”
records.arabicText: 349 - وَقَالَ أَمِيرُ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «دَخَلَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ عَلَى رَجُلٍ مِنْ وُلْدِ عَبْدِ اَلْمُطَّلِبِ وَ هُوَ فِي اَلسُّوقِ وَ قَدْ وُجِّهَ لِغَيْرِ اَلْقِبْلَةِ فَقَالَ «وَجِّهُوهُ إِلَى اَلْقِبْلَةِ فَإِنَّكُمْ إِذَا فَعَلْتُمْ ذَلِكَ أَقْبَلَتْ عَلَيْهِ اَلْمَلاَئِكَةُ وَ أَقْبَلَ اَللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ عَلَيْهِ بِوَجْهِهِ» فَلَمْ يَزَلْ كَذَلِكَ حَتَّى يُقْبَضَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.349 - The Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"The Messenger of Allah (sw) entered upon a man from the descendants of Abdul Muttalib, while he was in the throes of death and was facing away from the Qibla.
The Prophet said: 'Direct him toward the Qibla, for when you do so, the angels will approach him, and Allah (swt), the Mighty and Glorious, will turn toward him with His face (attention).'
And he remained in that state until he passed away.”

records.chapter: Washing of the Deceased
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.350 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"There is no one who is faced with death except that Iblis (Satan) sends some of his devils to entice him toward disbelief and to cause him to doubt his faith until his soul departs. Therefore, when you are present with your dying ones, instruct them to bear witness that there is no god but Allah (swt) and that Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah (sw) until they pass away."
records.arabicText: 350 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «مَا مِنْ أَحَدٍ يَحْضُرُهُ اَلْمَوْتُ إِلاَّ وَكَّلَ بِهِ إِبْلِيسُ مِنْ شَيَاطِينِهِ مَنْ يَأْمُرُهُ بِالْكُفْرِ وَ يُشَكِّكُهُ فِي دِينِهِ حَتَّى يَخْرُجَ نَفْسُهُ فَإِذَا حَضَرْتُمْ مَوْتَاكُمْ فَلَقِّنُوهُمْ شَهَادَةَ أَنْ لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اَللَّهُ وَ أَنَّ مُحَمَّداً رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ حَتَّى يَمُوتُوا.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.350 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no one who is faced with death except that Iblis (Satan) sends some of his devils to entice him toward disbelief and to cause him to doubt his faith until his soul departs. Therefore, when you are present with your dying ones, instruct them to bear witness that there is no god but Allah (swt) and that Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah (sw) until they pass away."

records.chapter: Washing of the Deceased
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.351 - The Messenger of Allah (sw) said in his last sermon:
"Whoever repents a year before his death, Allah (swt) will accept his repentance."
Then He (sw) said: "A year is a long time; whoever repents a month before their death, Allah (swt) will accept their repentance."
Then He (sw) said: "A month is a long time; whoever repents a week before their death, Allah (swt) will accept their repentance."
Then He (sw) said: "A week is a long time; whoever repents a day before their death, Allah (swt) will accept their repentance."
Then He (sw) said: "A day is a long time; whoever repents an hour before their death, Allah (swt) will accept their repentance."
Then He (sw) said: “An hour is a long time; whoever repents before their soul reaches here" — and he pointed to his throat — "Allah (swt) will accept their repentance”.
records.arabicText: 351 - وَقَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ فِي آخِرِ خُطْبَةٍ خَطَبَهَا: «مَنْ تَابَ قَبْلَ مَوْتِهِ بِسَنَةٍ تَابَ اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ» ثُمَّ قَالَ «إِنَّ اَلسَّنَةَ لَكَثِيرَةٌ مَنْ تَابَ قَبْلَ مَوْتِهِ بِشَهْرٍ تَابَ اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ» ثُمَّ قَالَ «إِنَّ اَلشَّهْرَ لَكَثِيرٌ وَ مَنْ تَابَ قَبْلَ مَوْتِهِ بِجُمْعَةٍ تَابَ اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ» ثُمَّ قَالَ «إِنَّ اَلْجُمْعَةَ لَكَثِيرَةٌ وَ مَنْ تَابَ قَبْلَ مَوْتِهِ بِيَوْمٍ تَابَ اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ» ثُمَّ قَالَ «وَ إِنَّ يَوْماً لَكَثِيرٌ وَ مَنْ تَابَ قَبْلَ مَوْتِهِ بِسَاعَةٍ تَابَ اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ» ثُمَّ قَالَ «وَ إِنَّ اَلسَّاعَةَ لَكَثِيرَةٌ وَ مَنْ تَابَ وَ قَدْ بَلَغَتْ نَفْسُهُ هَذِهِ» وَ أَهْوَى بِيَدِهِ إِلَى حَلْقِهِ «تَابَ اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.351 - The Messenger of Allah (sw) said in his last sermon:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Whoever repents a year before his death, Allah (swt) will accept his repentance."
Then He (sw) said: "A year is a long time; whoever repents a month before their death, Allah (swt) will accept their repentance."
Then He (sw) said: "A month is a long time; whoever repents a week before their death, Allah (swt) will accept their repentance."
Then He (sw) said: "A week is a long time; whoever repents a day before their death, Allah (swt) will accept their repentance."
Then He (sw) said: "A day is a long time; whoever repents an hour before their death, Allah (swt) will accept their repentance."
Then He (sw) said: “An hour is a long time; whoever repents before their soul reaches here" — and he pointed to his throat — "Allah (swt) will accept their repentance”.
